scope.summary: Extend the USD garment export (MT-250) to embed Chaos Cloth simulation-property attributes on the UsdGeomMesh so Unreal Engine 5 Chaos Cloth can re-simulate the garment using Tailor's fabric parameters without manual re-setup
acceptance_criteria: USD export with flag chaos_cloth=true writes UE-namespaced custom attributes on the exported UsdGeomMesh: unreal:clothConfig_MaxDistances, unreal:clothConfig_CollisionThickness, unreal:clothConfig_Friction derived from the garment's FabricProperties
Shrinkage values (weft/warp) from MaterialKeyframe tracks are baked as time-sample attributes on the USD stage so Chaos Cloth can read them per-frame
The export recipe documents the expected Unreal Engine import workflow (enable Chaos Cloth simulation on the imported USD skeletal mesh) and lists the attribute mapping table
The usd-export feature flag remains required; builds without it still produce OBJ fallback
A validation check CHAOS_CLOTH_ATTRS_PRESENT (Advisory) warns if the USD export is requested with chaos_cloth=true but shrinkage/collision data is absent from the garment spec
scope.risk_if_missed: Without Chaos Cloth property export, garments exported to Unreal Engine lose all physical-property context. Artists must manually re-configure cloth simulation in UE, negating the value of Tailor's anisotropic fabric model and keyframeable properties for real-time production pipelines.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
